title: Acer leiopodum
taxonomy: Acer leiopodum, also known as the thread-bark maple, is a species of maple tree within the genus Acer. The genus Acer is part of the family Sapindaceae, which is a family of flowering plants. Sapindaceae is a part of the larger order Sapindales, which includes other plants such as soapberry and lychee. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and evolutionary relationships. The thread-bark maple is native to eastern Asia.

intro: Acer leiopodum, also known as the paperbark maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Asia, characterized by its distinctive papery bark and five-petaled white flowers. The health benefits of Acer leiopodum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Acer leiopodum is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, which are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
